extraction.one_line_summary: SCHMID Mauro won from 15-minute breakaway while BERNAL Egan attacked isolated EVENEPOEL in final 12km of gravel sectors, gaining 2:08.
tactical_patterns: Bernal attacked immediately upon hearing Evenepoel isolated news, demonstrating real-time radio intelligence exploitation with 12km remaining.
Ineos let breakaway gain 15 minutes on gravel stage, prioritizing GC damage over stage control—unusual risk tolerance.
Evenepoel's teammate Almeida failed to drop back despite isolation, revealing Deceuninck team orders breakdown under pressure.
Buchmann bridged solo from favorites group to breakaway remnants, showing superior gravel handling despite climbing specialist profile.

rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: Sprinted through finish line despite guaranteed GC gains, showing dominance mentality and exceptional gravel form
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: Lost 2:08 while isolated and pulled earpiece in frustration—mental fragility under adversity exposed
rider_signals.ALMEIDA Joao: negative: Failed to drop back to assist isolated GC teammate Evenepoel despite team need
rider_signals.BUCHMANN Emanuel: positive: Attacked clear of favorites group and bridged to breakaway, unexpected gravel strength for pure climber
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: negative: Lost 23 seconds to Bernal despite being in same group until final kilometers
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: negative: Conceded 32 seconds to Bernal from same group, cracking on final climb
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: Dropped when Buchmann accelerated, concerning weakness on specialty terrain
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: neutral: Nearly crashed while pacing Bernal on gravel right-hander, overpaced for conditions
